content:
Hi, I'm trying to compile the current rust branch from the github repo on ubuntu 22.04 with WSL.
I've had success by literally replacing all symlinks in /usr/bin as the llvm-11 packages won't set themself as default (clang vs clang-11), and otherwise the makefile won't use the right version.

Now the problem that persists is that my bindgen libclang is apparently 15.0.2 but my clang version 11.0. I did use the quickstart commands to install the right bindgen and rustc version. I'd appreciate any hints. (I'm not even sure if the build succeeded, it finished, but if I re-run the make command, it starts to complain again about the clang versions, otherwise it looks like it's fine?)

You should be able to use LIBCLANG_PATH to specify which one is picked, for details you may want to check:

https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys#linking
https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys#dependencies
https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys#environment-variables
https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-bindgen#environment-variables

Current output:

# make LLVM=1 -j16
  DESCEND objtool
  CALL    scripts/atomic/check-atomics.sh
  CALL    scripts/checksyscalls.sh
***
*** libclang (used by the Rust bindings generator 'bindgen')
*** version does not match Clang's. This may be a problem.
***   libclang version: 15.0.2
***   Clang version:    11.0.0
***
  CHK     include/generated/compile.h
Kernel: arch/x86/boot/bzImage is ready  (#1)

if I re-run the make command, it starts to complain again about the clang versions, otherwise it looks like it's fine?

If you mean it prints the warnings, that should be "fine" (i.e. the version may be wrong, but it may still work).
Of course, it is best if you solve the issue and make it use the same version to avoid possible problems.

ok I so set

export LIBCLANG_PATH=/usr/lib/llvm-11/lib/libclang
export CLANG_PATH=/usr/lib/llvm-11/bin/clang

Now I get

make LLVM=1 rustavailable
./scripts/rust_is_available.sh: 21: arithmetic expression: expecting primary: "100000 *  + 100 *  + "
make: *** [Makefile:1812: rustavailable] Error 2

With rustc --version: rustc 1.62.0 (a8314ef7d 2022-06-27)
Ok so the fix it to not use any env stuff. Just make LLVM=-11
I usually compile with the lib clang warning as well, as I use bindgen from my os. Never had any problems with that.

Yeah I got it working by simply ignoring any compatibility checks and using default llvm.
No more problems of llvm-11 not aliasing to llvm and then the same stuff with ld and all other libs.. Just ignore that and move on, it'll work.
